rows.notes: Top-of-page title/header. Fully legible, centered left text, no monetary values in the right columns.

Amount columns well aligned with vertical rules. Pounds '8' and shillings '12' are clear; pence shows '0' with a fractional mark reading as '3/4' (interpreted as 0.75). Minor uncertainty on the small superscript fraction stroke, but placement and shape consistent with 3/4.

Description legible. Right-hand columns aligned; reading is 3 pounds, 0 shillings, 5 pence. Small tail after the pence digit interpreted as '1/2' (0.5). Handwriting of fraction clear enough to distinguish from 1/4 or 3/4.

Amount columns align to show 22 pounds, 1 shilling, 7 pence (no visible fraction). The surname on the left is somewhat unclear (letters could be 'Trekwin', 'Trehwin' or similar); chosen reading 'Trehwin - Hodge' based on letter shapes but with moderate uncertainty.
Short place/section label with no amounts in the columns. Text is legible and appears to be a grouping label rather than a monetary entry.

Amounts read clearly as 20 pounds, 10 shillings, 3 pence. The description is faint/partly ambiguous (handwritten word after 'Taylor' is unclear — transcribed as 'alfo' to reflect appearance); moderate uncertainty on the textual part but numeric columns are well aligned and clear.

Description reads as 'Judge' (letters clear). Amount columns aligned and read as 10 pounds, 14 shillings, 1 pence with a half-penny marker (interpreted as 1/2 → 0.5). Minor ambiguity on whether the first letter is 'J' or 'T', but context and shape favor 'Judge'.

Amounts read as 0 pounds, 10 shillings, 10 pence. The left-hand name is somewhat indistinct (could be 'Alton' or similar) — transcribed as 'Altons.' to match appearance. Numbers are aligned and within valid ranges.
Name 'Hartley' is legible. Amounts read 2 pounds, 0 shillings, 2 pence with a fractional mark '3/4' (interpreted as 0.75). Fraction shape and position are clear.
Name and numeric columns clearly legible. Read as 23 pounds, 12 shillings, 5 pence; no fractional pence mark visible. All currency fields fall within valid ranges.

Amounts read as 25 pounds, 16 shillings, 0 pence with a small '1/4' mark (interpreted as 0.25). The parenthetical surname is faint/uncertain — transcribed as '(Worley)' to reflect the visible characters; moderate uncertainty on the exact spelling but numeric columns are clear.

rows.description: Stipend or Quit-Rent for Kidlington Vicarage vid. Am: pro 2d die. Novr. 1810. mem: +
rows.notes: Long explanatory/footer note at bottom left of the entries. No monetary columns populated. Several abbreviations and small-letter words (e.g. 'vid.', 'Am:', 'pro', '2d', 'die', 'Novr.') are somewhat faint; transcription chosen to reflect probable meaning but textual uncertainty remains.
